entities.name: The Parties
entities.legal_role: The Parties are the contractual entities obligated to maintain accurate records and comply with billing procedures under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: The Parties will maintain accurate records of all direct and indirect costs and expenses incurred in the performance of the Services under this Agreement.

entities.name: A Provider
entities.legal_role: The Provider is the party responsible for rendering Services and submitting monthly invoices or reports for costs and expenses incurred.
entities.evidence_quote: A Provider providing any such Services shall render a monthly invoice or accounting system generated report summarizing the direct and allocated costs and expenses for that month.

entities.name: Services
entities.legal_role: The Services are the subject matter of the Agreement for which costs and expenses are incurred and billed.
entities.evidence_quote: costs and expenses incurred in the performance of the Services under this Agreement

entities.name: Monthly invoice or accounting system generated report
entities.legal_role: The monthly invoice or report is the formal billing document submitted by the Provider to summarize costs and expenses for approval and payment.
entities.evidence_quote: A Provider providing any such Services shall render a monthly invoice or accounting system generated report summarizing the direct and allocated costs and expenses for that month.

entities.name: Company
entities.legal_role: The Company is the receiving Party entitled to request supporting documentation and reimburse the Provider for out-of-pocket expenses.
entities.evidence_quote: shall provide supporting documentation of such payments as requested by Company.

entities.name: third parties
entities.legal_role: Third parties are external vendors or suppliers from whom products or services are procured under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: pays third parties for products or services provided under this Agreement

entities.name: reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses
entities.legal_role: These expenses are costs actually incurred by the Provider on behalf of the receiving Party and are subject to reimbursement upon documentation.
entities.evidence_quote: the Provider shall be reimbursed for any and all reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred

relationships.legal_significance: The Provider is legally obligated to submit monthly invoices or reports summarizing costs and expenses to enable review and approval of charges.
relationships.evidence_quote: A Provider providing any such Services shall render a monthly invoice or accounting system generated report summarizing the direct and allocated costs and expenses for that month.

relationships.legal_significance: The Parties must review and approve the monthly invoices or reports to ensure charges are properly billed before payment.
relationships.evidence_quote: Each monthly invoice or accounting system generated report will be reviewed before approval of the charges

relationships.legal_significance: The Provider may act as a paying agent for third parties on behalf of the receiving Party, incurring reimbursable expenses.
relationships.evidence_quote: In cases in which a Provider renders no Services itself but merely pays third parties for products or services provided under this Agreement (as a matter of convenience to the receiving Party)

relationships.legal_significance: The Provider has the right to be reimbursed for reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred when paying third parties.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Provider shall be reimbursed for any and all reasonable, ordinary, and necessary out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred

relationships.legal_significance: The Provider must provide supporting documentation of payments to third parties upon request by the Company to substantiate reimbursement claims.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall provide supporting documentation of such payments as requested by Company.

extractor_notes: The clause clearly distinguishes between Providers who perform Services and those who only pay third parties, establishing distinct billing and reimbursement obligations. The term 'Company' is used as the receiving Party entitled to request documentation, implying a defined party role within the Agreement.

entities.name: Company
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the Provider and its Affiliates against third-party liabilities arising from the Company's failure to perform under the Agreement, except for specified exclusions.
entities.evidence_quote: Company shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Provider or its Affiliates by a third party arising as a result of or in any way connected with the Company’s failure to perform under this Agreement

entities.name: Provider
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the Company against third-party liabilities arising from the Provider’s or its Affiliates’ failure to perform under the Agreement, except for specified exclusions.
entities.evidence_quote: Provider shall, to the fullest extent permitted by Applicable Law, defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Company by a third party arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) failure to perform under this Agreement

entities.name: Provider and its Affiliates
entities.legal_role: Entities protected by the Company’s indemnification obligations against third-party claims related to the Company’s failure to perform.
entities.evidence_quote: defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Provider or its Affiliates by a third party

entities.name: Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’)
entities.legal_role: Entities whose failure to perform triggers Provider’s indemnification obligations to the Company.
entities.evidence_quote: arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) failure to perform under this Agreement

entities.name: Indemnified Party
entities.legal_role: A party receiving notice or knowledge of a claim that may result in an indemnification claim under Section 7.2.
entities.evidence_quote: If any Party hereto (each, an “Indemnified Party”) shall receive notice or have knowledge of any claim that may result in a claim for indemnification by such Indemnified Party against a Party pursuant to this Section 7.2

entities.name: Indemnifying Party
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to indemnify the Indemnified Party upon notice of a claim, including the right to assume defense and control of legal proceedings.
entities.evidence_quote: the indemnifying Party shall, upon its acknowledgement in writing of its obligation to indemnify the Indemnified Party seeking indemnification, be entitled to assume the defense or to represent the interests of the Indemnified Party

entities.name: Liabilities
entities.legal_role: Claims or asserted liabilities by third parties that trigger indemnification obligations under this clause.
entities.evidence_quote: any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Provider or its Affiliates by a third party ... any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Company by a third party

entities.name: Exceptions to Indemnification
entities.legal_role: Specified circumstances (fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, gross negligence) that exclude indemnification obligations for the failing party.
entities.evidence_quote: except to the extent such failure was the result of fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence on the part of Provider ... except to the extent such Liabilities are caused by fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence by Company

relationships.legal_significance: Company is legally obligated to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates against third-party claims arising from the Company’s failure to perform under the Agreement, except for specified exceptions.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company shall, to the fullest extent permitted by law, defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Provider and its Affiliates from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Provider or its Affiliates by a third party arising as a result of or in any way connected with the Company’s failure to perform under this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Provider is legally obligated to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company against third-party claims arising from Provider’s or its Affiliates’ failure to perform under the Agreement, except for specified exceptions.
relationships.evidence_quote: Provider shall, to the fullest extent permitted by Applicable Law, defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Company from and against any and all Liabilities claimed or asserted against Company by a third party arising as a result of Provider’s (or any of its Affiliates’) failure to perform under this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: The Indemnified Party must promptly notify the Indemnifying Party of claims, enabling the Indemnifying Party to assume defense and control of the claim, which is critical for managing legal risk and costs.
relationships.evidence_quote: such Indemnified Party shall, as promptly as possible, give the indemnifying Party notice of such claim ... The Party against whom indemnification is claimed shall, upon its acknowledgement in writing of its obligation to indemnify the Indemnified Party seeking indemnification, be entitled to assume the defense or to represent the interests of the Indemnified Party

relationships.legal_significance: The Indemnifying Party has the right to select counsel, direct defense, and propose or accept settlements on behalf of the Indemnified Party, subject to the Indemnified Party’s consent for nonmonetary relief, which affects control over litigation and settlement outcomes.
relationships.evidence_quote: be entitled to assume the defense or to represent the interests of the Indemnified Party ... which shall include the right to select and direct legal counsel and other consultants ... to propose, accept, or reject offers of settlement ... provided, however, that without the Indemnified Party’s consent ... the indemnifying Party may not consent to entry of a judgment or settlement if such judgment or settlement provides for injunctive or other nonmonetary relief affecting the Indemnified Party

relationships.legal_significance: The Company is not obligated to indemnify the Provider for liabilities resulting from the Provider’s fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence, limiting the scope of indemnification.
relationships.evidence_quote: except to the extent such failure was the result of fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence on the part of Provider

relationships.legal_significance: The Provider is not obligated to indemnify the Company for liabilities caused by the Company’s fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence, limiting the scope of indemnification.
relationships.evidence_quote: except to the extent such Liabilities are caused by fraud, willful misconduct, violation of Applicable Law, or gross negligence by Company

extractor_notes: The clause establishes reciprocal indemnification obligations between Company and Provider, with detailed procedures for notice, defense, and settlement control, and includes standard carve-outs for fraud, willful misconduct, violation of law, and gross negligence. The clause carefully balances control over defense and settlement with the Indemnified Party's consent rights for nonmonetary relief.

source_clause_title: 10.2 Assignment.   The rights under this Agreement shall not be assignable or

entities.name: the Parties
entities.legal_role: The Parties are the primary obligors and beneficiaries under the Agreement, whose rights and duties are governed by the clause.
entities.evidence_quote: any Person or entity, other than the Parties hereto and their permitted successors-in-interest and permitted assignees, any rights or remedies under or by reason of this Agreement

entities.name: permitted successors-in-interest
entities.legal_role: Permitted successors-in-interest are entities legally entitled to succeed the Parties and assume their rights and obligations under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: the Parties hereto and their permitted successors-in-interest and permitted assignees

entities.name: permitted assignees
entities.legal_role: Permitted assignees are entities authorized to receive rights and obligations under the Agreement through assignment.
entities.evidence_quote: the Parties hereto and their permitted successors-in-interest and permitted assignees

entities.name: Secured Party
entities.legal_role: A Secured Party is a creditor or entity granted a security interest in a Party's rights under the Agreement to secure obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: Company or Provider may grant to a Secured Party a security interest in its rights under this Agreement

entities.name: Company or Provider
entities.legal_role: Company or Provider is a Party to the Agreement who may grant security interests and assign rights under specified conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: Company or Provider may grant to a Secured Party a security interest in its rights under this Agreement

entities.name: successor of the assigning Party
entities.legal_role: A successor Affiliate is an entity that may receive an assignment of the Agreement rights from a Party, subject to notice and conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: to the successor of the assigning Party, if such successor is an Affiliate of such Party

entities.name: Affiliate of the assigning Party
entities.legal_role: An Affiliate is an entity related to a Party that may acquire all or controlling interest in the Party's business assets and receive an assignment.
entities.evidence_quote: to an Affiliate of the assigning Party acquiring all or a controlling interest in the business assets of such Party

relationships.legal_significance: This relationship allows the Company or Provider to secure obligations by granting a security interest in its Agreement rights to a Secured Party, without releasing obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company or Provider may grant to a Secured Party a security interest in its rights under this Agreement, provided that neither the grant of any such interest, nor the foreclosure of any such interest, shall in any way release, reduce, or diminish the obligations of such Party hereunder.

relationships.legal_significance: The Company or Provider may assign the Agreement to a successor Affiliate with 90 days prior written notice, enabling continuity of rights and obligations within affiliated entities.
relationships.evidence_quote: This Agreement may be assigned by a Party, with ninety (90) days’ prior written notice to the other Parties: (i) to the successor of the assigning Party, if such successor is an Affiliate of such Party

relationships.relationship_type: Assignment to Affiliate Acquiring Business Assets
relationships.legal_significance: The Company or Provider may assign the Agreement to an Affiliate acquiring all or controlling interest in its business assets, ensuring the transfer of contractual rights and duties upon such acquisition.
relationships.evidence_quote: This Agreement may be assigned by a Party, with ninety (90) days’ prior written notice to the other Parties: ... (ii) to an Affiliate of the assigning Party acquiring all or a controlling interest in the business assets of such Party.

relationships.legal_significance: Upon security assignment, the assigning Party must execute a consent and agreement for the benefit of the Secured Party, formalizing the security interest and protecting the Secured Party's rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: In the event of such security assignment, the assigning Party will execute for the benefit of such Secured Party a consent and agreement.

relationships.relationship_type: Consent Requirement for Assignment or Delegation
relationships.legal_significance: The Parties must provide prior written consent for any assignment or delegation of rights or duties, which cannot be unreasonably withheld or delayed, ensuring control over contractual relationships.
relationships.evidence_quote: The rights under this Agreement shall not be assignable or transferrable nor the duties delegable by any Party without prior written consent of the other Parties, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly prohibits assignment or delegation without prior written consent, but provides specific exceptions for security interests and assignments to Affiliates or successors with notice. The clause also clarifies that security interests do not diminish obligations. The legal entities and relationships are clearly defined with no ambiguity.

entities.name: Each Party
entities.legal_role: A Party is a signatory to the Agreement obligated to maintain confidentiality and abide by the terms of the contract.
entities.evidence_quote: Each Party shall keep any information not intended for public disclosure and considered to be confidential or proprietary...

entities.name: Confidential Information
entities.legal_role: Confidential Information is the protected data or knowledge that Parties must keep secret and not disclose or misuse under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: information relating to the interconnection of customers to a utility's transmission or distribution systems, proprietary customer information, trade secrets, competitive information, and information about a utility's transmission or distribution system, operations, or plans for expansion (“Confidential Information”)

entities.name: Disclosing Party
entities.legal_role: The Party who provides Confidential Information to another Party and whose rights to confidentiality must be protected.
entities.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose another Party’s Confidential Information to others... unless... the receiving Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party.

entities.name: Receiving Party
entities.legal_role: The Party who receives Confidential Information and is bound to keep it confidential and use it only as permitted under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose another Party’s Confidential Information to others, nor shall a Party use the other Party’s Confidential Information for its own benefit... unless... the receiving Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party.

entities.name: Governmental Authority
entities.legal_role: An official body that may validly order or request disclosure of Confidential Information under applicable law.
entities.evidence_quote: Confidential Information may be disclosed by a receiving Party in response to a valid order or request of a Governmental Authority...

entities.name: Applicable Law
entities.legal_role: The legal framework that may require disclosure of Confidential Information despite confidentiality obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: or if it is otherwise required to be disclosed by Applicable Law...

entities.name: Internal Document Retention Policies
entities.legal_role: Policies that may permit the Receiving Party to retain Confidential Information for legal, corporate governance, or regulatory compliance purposes.
entities.evidence_quote: Confidential Information may be retained by the receiving Party to the extent that retention of such Confidential Information is necessary to comply with the receiving Party’s internal document retention policies aimed at legal, corporate governance, or regulatory compliance...

entities.name: Certification by Authorized Officer
entities.legal_role: A written certification by a duly authorized officer of the Receiving Party confirming compliance with return or destruction obligations of Confidential Information.
entities.evidence_quote: The receiving Party shall, upon written request of the disclosing Party, cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the disclosing Party that the requirements... have been satisfied in full.

entities.name: Equitable Relief
entities.legal_role: A remedy available to the Disclosing Party to prevent or stop violations of confidentiality obligations, including injunctive relief.
entities.evidence_quote: the disclosing Party is entitled to seek immediate equitable relief including injunctive relief prohibiting any violation of this Section 4.2...

relationships.legal_significance: Each Party is legally obligated to keep Confidential Information secret and not disclose or misuse it, ensuring protection of sensitive data.
relationships.evidence_quote: Each Party shall keep any information... considered to be confidential or proprietary... in strict confidence at all times...

relationships.relationship_type: Duty to Obtain Prior Written Consent
relationships.legal_significance: The Receiving Party must obtain the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent before disclosing or using Confidential Information beyond permitted purposes.
relationships.evidence_quote: no Party shall disclose another Party’s Confidential Information... unless... the receiving Party obtains the prior written consent of the disclosing Party.

relationships.relationship_type: Permitted Disclosure under Valid Governmental Order
relationships.legal_significance: Confidential Information may be disclosed if required by a valid order or request of a Governmental Authority, providing a lawful exception to confidentiality.
relationships.evidence_quote: Confidential Information may be disclosed by a receiving Party in response to a valid order or request of a Governmental Authority...

relationships.legal_significance: Applicable Law may require disclosure of Confidential Information, creating a legal exception to confidentiality obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: or if it is otherwise required to be disclosed by Applicable Law...

relationships.legal_significance: The Receiving Party may retain Confidential Information to comply with internal policies related to legal, governance, or regulatory compliance, subject to confidentiality restrictions.
relationships.evidence_quote: Confidential Information may be retained by the receiving Party to the extent that retention... is necessary to comply with the receiving Party’s internal document retention policies...

relationships.relationship_type: Certification Obligation upon Return or Destruction
relationships.legal_significance: Upon written request, the Receiving Party must have an authorized officer certify in writing that Confidential Information has been returned or destroyed as required.
relationships.evidence_quote: The receiving Party shall... cause one of its duly authorized officers to certify in writing to the disclosing Party that the requirements... have been satisfied in full.

relationships.legal_significance: The Disclosing Party has the right to seek immediate equitable remedies, including injunctive relief, to prevent or stop breaches of confidentiality.
relationships.evidence_quote: the disclosing Party is entitled to seek immediate equitable relief including injunctive relief prohibiting any violation of this Section 4.2...

extractor_notes: The clause comprehensively defines Confidential Information and sets strict confidentiality obligations on the Parties, including exceptions for legal requirements and internal compliance. It also establishes procedural rights such as certification of destruction and equitable remedies for breaches.

entities.name: Providers
entities.legal_role: Providers are the parties obligated to comply with the Company's Code of Conduct and to provide services under the contract, making them subject to specific conduct and training requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: Providers shall, at the Company’s expense, (i) comply with the Company’s instructions relating to adherence to Company’s Code of Conduct...

entities.name: Company
entities.legal_role: The Company is the party receiving services from Providers and is responsible for instructing Providers on the Code of Conduct and ensuring compliance with applicable laws and training.
entities.evidence_quote: Providers shall, at the Company’s expense, (i) comply with the Company’s instructions relating to adherence to Company’s Code of Conduct...

entities.name: Company’s Code of Conduct
entities.legal_role: The Company’s Code of Conduct is a binding set of behavioral and compliance standards that Providers must adhere to when performing services.
entities.evidence_quote: comply with the Company’s instructions relating to adherence to Company’s Code of Conduct, if any, (a copy of which has been or will be delivered to Providers)...

entities.name: Provider’s Code of Conduct
entities.legal_role: The Provider’s Code of Conduct is a binding set of behavioral and compliance standards that the Company must adhere to when receiving services from Providers.
entities.evidence_quote: Company shall, at the Provider’s expense, (i) comply with each Provider’s instructions relating to adherence to the Provider’s Code of Conduct (a copy of which has been or will be delivered to Company)...

entities.name: jurisdictional Governmental Authority
entities.legal_role: A governmental authority that may impose additional Code of Conduct requirements on either the Company or Providers, triggering compliance obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: work with the Company to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon Company by a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by Applicable Law...

entities.name: Applicable Law
entities.legal_role: Applicable Law refers to the legal requirements that may impose additional Code of Conduct obligations on the Company or Providers.
entities.evidence_quote: work with the Company to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon Company by a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by Applicable Law...

entities.name: Training in Code of Conduct requirements
entities.legal_role: A contractual obligation requiring employees of Providers or Company to receive training on the applicable Code of Conduct, including handling confidential information and avoiding inappropriate cross-subsidization.
entities.evidence_quote: at Company’s request, ensure that each employee of Providers that are providing Services receives training in the Company’s Code of Conduct requirements, including training with respect to handling of confidential information and avoidance of inappropriate cross subsidization...

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Comply with Code of Conduct
relationships.legal_significance: Providers are contractually obligated to comply with the Company’s Code of Conduct, which governs their conduct while providing services.
relationships.evidence_quote: Providers shall, at the Company’s expense, (i) comply with the Company’s instructions relating to adherence to Company’s Code of Conduct...

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Comply with Code of Conduct
relationships.legal_significance: The Company is contractually obligated to comply with the Provider’s Code of Conduct when receiving services, ensuring mutual adherence to conduct standards.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company shall, at the Provider’s expense, (i) comply with each Provider’s instructions relating to adherence to the Provider’s Code of Conduct...

relationships.relationship_type: Duty to Inform of Additional Code of Conduct Requirements
relationships.legal_significance: The Company must inform Providers of any additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed by Governmental Authorities or Applicable Law, enabling Providers to comply accordingly.
relationships.evidence_quote: work with the Company to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon Company by a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by Applicable Law (provided Company informs Providers of such additional requirements)...

relationships.relationship_type: Duty to Inform of Additional Code of Conduct Requirements
relationships.legal_significance: Providers must inform the Company of any additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed by Governmental Authorities or Applicable Law, enabling the Company to comply accordingly.
relationships.evidence_quote: work with the Provider to comply with all additional Code of Conduct requirements imposed upon Provider by a jurisdictional Governmental Authority or by other Applicable Law (provided Provider informs Company of such additional requirements)...

relationships.legal_significance: The Company has the right to require Providers to train their employees on the Company’s Code of Conduct, ensuring compliance and proper handling of confidential information.
relationships.evidence_quote: at Company’s request, ensure that each employee of Providers that are providing Services receives training in the Company’s Code of Conduct requirements...

relationships.legal_significance: Providers have the right to require the Company to train its employees on the Provider’s Code of Conduct, ensuring compliance and proper handling of confidential information.
relationships.evidence_quote: at the Provider’s request, ensure that each employee of Company receiving Services receives training in the Providers’ applicable Code of Conduct requirements...

relationships.legal_significance: The Company is responsible for paying the expenses related to Providers’ compliance with the Company’s Code of Conduct and related training.
relationships.evidence_quote: Providers shall, at the Company’s expense, (i) comply with the Company’s instructions...

relationships.legal_significance: Providers are responsible for paying the expenses related to the Company’s compliance with the Provider’s Code of Conduct and related training.
relationships.evidence_quote: Company shall, at the Provider’s expense, (i) comply with each Provider’s instructions...

extractor_notes: The clause establishes reciprocal obligations between the Company and Providers to comply with each other's Codes of Conduct, including mutual rights to require employee training and reciprocal payment obligations for compliance costs. The clause also includes a duty to inform the other party of additional legal or governmental Code of Conduct requirements, ensuring ongoing compliance.

entities.legal_role: The Allocated Charge is the monetary amount billed for the Services, subject to verification and adjustment if inconsistent with the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: the allocated charge billed for the Services is not consistent with this Agreement

entities.name: overpayment or underpayment
entities.legal_role: Payment Discrepancy represents any monetary difference identified by audit that must be corrected by prompt payment between the Parties.
entities.evidence_quote: such that an overpayment or an underpayment has been made, the amount of such overpayment or underpayment shall promptly be paid to the Party to which it is owed by the other Party

entities.name: period being reviewed
entities.legal_role: The Audit Period limits the timeframe for which records may be audited, ensuring audits cover no more than three years prior to initiation.
entities.evidence_quote: the period being reviewed shall be no more than three years prior to the date of the initiation of the audit

relationships.legal_significance: The Parties have the contractual right to engage independent auditors at their own expense to verify compliance, which enables enforcement of audit rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Parities shall have the right, at their expense, to engage independent auditors to perform a periodic audit

relationships.legal_significance: Independent auditors are authorized to audit the records and books related to the Services, ensuring transparency and compliance with contractual terms.
relationships.evidence_quote: to perform a periodic audit of the records and books for the Services provided pursuant to this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Auditors verify that the Services and related billing comply with Applicable Law, which is essential for lawful performance and risk mitigation.
relationships.evidence_quote: to verify compliance with Applicable Law

relationships.legal_significance: The audit must be conducted timely, limiting the review period to no more than three years prior to audit initiation, which restricts retroactive liability.
relationships.evidence_quote: Any audit to be conducted shall be performed timely such that the period being reviewed shall be no more than three years prior to the date of the initiation of the audit

relationships.legal_significance: If the allocated charge is inconsistent with the Agreement, it triggers a payment discrepancy that must be promptly corrected between the Parties.
relationships.evidence_quote: If any audit or review of a Party’s books and records discloses that the allocated charge billed for the Services is not consistent with this Agreement such that an overpayment or an underpayment has been made

relationships.legal_significance: The Party owing the overpayment or underpayment must promptly pay the other Party, ensuring financial reconciliation and enforcement of payment terms.
relationships.evidence_quote: the amount of such overpayment or underpayment shall promptly be paid to the Party to which it is owed by the other Party

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines audit rights, audit scope, time limitations, and payment reconciliation obligations without ambiguity. The term 'Parities' appears to be a typographical error for 'Parties'.
